body_txt: In most time travel/cloning/alternate reality stories, one character can have multiple copies of him- or herself running around in the same time period. Sometimes, there might be problems if those copies meet each other . Other times, a character's future self can change their past self's behavior, creating a second self . Then there are these cases... Only One Me Allowed Right Now is a case where either the Universe flat out denies multiple copies of a character to exist in a same time period, the character and the copies go crazy, the Universe starts to break down , or something else bad happens. Note that this is not Never the Selves Shall Meet . In that one, you can have millions of copies of a same character running around in the same universe without that much trouble, only they must not meet each other. In this case, even though there are only two copies and they are at opposite ends of the Universe, the problem still happens. Not as tightly related to the One-Steve Limit as you might think. Sub-Trope of Our Clones Are Different . Interestingly enough, this doesn't happen in real-life.
